findings.evidence: PR line 117 has `(c₀ : ℝ)`, while existing field-level energy lemmas use `{c : X → ℝ}` and evaluate at `c x`, e.g. `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:72-78` and `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/EnergyForm.lean:221-227`.
findings.fix: Generalize the theorem to `{c : X → ℝ}` and conclude `energyIntegrand (a x) 0 (c x) U V = energyIntegrand (a x) 0 (c x) V U`; derive the constant version only if it is still needed.
findings.issue: `energyIntegrand_zero_drift_comm_on` fixes the mass term as a constant `c₀ : ℝ`, although symmetry imposes no condition on the mass and the surrounding `_on` API works with coefficient fields.

summary: The pointwise symmetry lemmas are mostly at the right level, but the domain-level theorem unnecessarily specializes the mass coefficient to a constant. That is below the natural coefficient-field API used by the surrounding energy-form files.
